sections.heading: 🌍 Global Market Briefing — Asian Session
sections.content: Risk sentiment remains cautiously constructive as markets digest continued central bank divergence, with the Fed's hawkish stance supporting USD strength while European equities outperform on ECB dovish expectations. Asian markets face headwinds from Japan's ongoing currency intervention concerns as USD/JPY tests 158.50, while commodity currencies weaken on mixed China data. Energy complex strength contrasts with precious metals selloff as real yields climb.

sections.heading: 📊 FOREX — Currency Strength
sections.content:
1. USD (+0.31% avg) - Safe haven demand, Fed premium intact
2. CHF (+0.31%) - European flight-to-quality flows
3. JPY (+0.12%) - Intervention fears cap upside despite weakness
4. EUR (-0.18%) - ECB dovish repricing continues
5. GBP (-0.34%) - BoE pause expectations weigh
6. CAD (-0.23%) - Oil strength offset by risk-off flows
7. AUD (-0.68%) - China concerns, RBA dovish pivot
8. NZD (-0.69%) - RBNZ easing cycle momentum

sections.heading: 💱 Key Currency Pairs
sections.content:
EUR/USD 1.1650: Bearish bias intact below 1.1680 resistance. ECB-Fed divergence narrative driving flows toward 1.1600 support. European political uncertainty adds downside pressure.
USD/JPY 158.5350: Critical juncture as intervention zone approaches. BoJ verbal warnings intensifying but Fed hawkishness supports further upside toward 159.00. Volatility elevated.
GBP/USD 1.3356: Weakness accelerating below 1.3400 as UK inflation concerns ease. Technical breakdown suggests 1.3300 test imminent with limited buying interest emerging.
AUD/USD 0.7174: China growth concerns and RBA dovish shift driving sustained selling. Break below 0.7200 opens path to 0.7100 with commodity headwinds persisting.

sections.heading: 💰 FX Flows & Positioning
sections.content:
• Real money USD buying accelerating across G10, particularly vs commodity bloc
• Japanese corporates increasing hedging activity above 158.00 in USD/JPY
• European pension funds reducing EUR exposure on ECB dovish repricing
• Systematic trend followers adding to USD long positions across majors

sections.heading: 📈 EQUITIES — Indices
sections.content: European markets lead with DAX +1.32%, CAC 40 +0.93% on dovish ECB expectations boosting growth sectors. US indices extend gains: S&P 500 $748.17 (+0.79%), Nasdaq 26635 (+0.88%), Dow 50063 (+0.75%) as tech momentum continues. Nikkei 225 61485 (-1.87%) underperforms on intervention concerns and yen strength fears. FTSE 100 10373 (+0.46%) lags on sterling weakness.

sections.heading: 🛢️ COMMODITIES
sections.content: Gold $4589.70 (-2.04%): Technical breakdown below $4650 as real yields climb. Next support $4550 with Fed premium weighing. Silver $80.58 (-5.56%): Amplified gold weakness, industrial demand concerns. WTI $102.61 (+1.42%): Supply concerns support despite demand questions. Natural Gas $2.914 (+0.69%): Storage dynamics and weather patterns supportive.

sections.heading: ₿ CRYPTO
sections.content: BTC $80401 (-0.82%): Consolidating below $81000 resistance. Support holding at $79500 with institutional flows mixed. ETH $2241.42 (-1.80%): ETH/BTC ratio weakening to 0.0279, underperforming amid staking concerns. XRP $1.4540 (-2.04%), SOL $90.42 (-1.88%), DOGE $0.1130 (-1.98%) all following broader risk-off sentiment in digital assets.

sections.heading: 🎯 Intraday Trade Ideas
sections.content:
📈 LONG — USD/CHF above 0.7860 targeting 0.7900, WTI crude on supply concerns, DAX on ECB dovish premium, Natural Gas seasonal support
📉 SHORT — AUD/USD below 0.7170 targeting 0.7100, Gold break of $4580, Silver momentum, EUR/GBP on relative policy divergence

sections.heading: 📅 Today's Macro Drivers
sections.content: US retail sales and industrial production data key for Fed pricing. ECB officials' commentary on policy outlook. China property and industrial data. 10Y Treasury yields testing 4.75% resistance level critical for FX flows.
